mnemonics: VLC: Very Low Credibility. A model that memorizes the training data (like a VLC playlist) performs perfectly on it but has low credibility (generalizes poorly) on new, unseen data.
keyPoints: Overfitting occurs when a model learns the training data too well, including its noise and random fluctuations.
An overfit model has high variance and low bias, performing excellently on training data but poorly on new, unseen data.
Techniques to prevent overfitting include cross-validation, regularization (L1/L2), pruning (for trees), and using more training data.
commonMistakes: Mistaking high training accuracy for a good model, without validating on a hold-out test set.
Applying too much regularization, which can lead to the opposite problem: underfitting.
